task_completion.description: Open Eclipse IDE and navigate to the LogisticsSystem project
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eclipse IDE is open, showing the 'LogisticsSystem' project in the Package Explorer or Project Explorer.

task_completion.description: Open the 'Push Down' refactoring dialog for the Transport class
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Push Down' is visible on screen, triggered from the Transport.java file.

task_completion.description: Select the correct method and field to push down
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Push Down' dialog, the checkboxes next to the 'checkTirePressure' method and the 'MAX_TIRE_PSI' field are checked.

task_completion.description: Verify the members are removed from the Transport superclass
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows Transport.java, and the 'checkTirePressure' method and 'MAX_TIRE_PSI' field are no longer present in the code.

task_completion.description: Verify the members are added to the Truck subclass
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows Truck.java, and it now contains the 'checkTirePressure()' method and 'MAX_TIRE_PSI' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the project builds successfully without errors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Problems view shows 0 errors, and there are no red 'x' error markers on the project files or folders in the Package Explorer.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE GUI to perform the refactoring
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Eclipse IDE interface, specifically the Refactor context menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the automated 'Push Down' refactoring tool rather than manual cut-and-paste
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Push Down' refactoring dialog is clearly visible in at least one screenshot.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external text editors to bypass Eclipse
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal-based text editors (like nano or vim) or simple GUI text editors are shown modifying the Java files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at '/home/ga/eclipse-workspace/LogisticsSystem'. The superclass is 'src/main/java/com/logistics/domain/Transport.java'. The target subclass is 'src/main/java/com/logistics/domain/Truck.java'. The method to move is 'public boolean checkTirePressure()' and the field is 'MAX_TIRE_PSI' (private static final int = 110). The refactoring must be done using Eclipse's 'Push Down' feature, ensuring these members are removed from Transport and added to Truck, without affecting sibling classes like Ship.
